description: Fix RTMP streaming failure — some video files cause Connection reset by peer after ~5 seconds when pushed via FFmpeg to nginx-rtmp. cam-2 (110kbps HE-AAC) works, cam-1 (258kbps AAC LC) fails. Must diagnose root cause and fix.

userStories.title: Diagnose and fix RTMP streaming failure for high-bitrate videos
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to find why some local mp4 files fail with 'Connection reset by peer' after ~5 seconds when FFmpeg pushes to nginx-rtmp, and fix it so all demo videos stream reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause identified and documented in progress.txt
Both yt-cam-1 and yt-cam-2 stream continuously without Connection reset by peer errors
Videos loop without interruption (FFmpeg restarts cleanly at video end)
docker compose up -d starts all services and both streams are visible within 30 seconds
Fix committed with clear explanation in commit message
userStories.notes: Known facts: cam-2 (110kbps, HE-AAC 47k) works. cam-1 (258kbps, AAC LC 128k) fails after ~5s. New video (300kbps) also fails. Error is always 'Connection reset by peer' on audio packet muxing. nginx-rtmp error log shows no RTMP-level errors. Happens with single stream too (not concurrency issue). nginx-rtmp config: chunk_size 4096, no explicit limits. Streaming container uses tiangolo/nginx-rtmp or similar alpine-based image. Things to investigate: 1) nginx-rtmp version/module compatibility with AAC LC vs HE-AAC 2) FLV container limitations for certain AAC profiles 3) chunk_size vs bitrate ratio 4) FFmpeg -re flag timing issues with higher bitrate 5) Try re-encoding audio to HE-AAC before streaming 6) Try removing -re flag 7) Check if -stream_loop works vs manual loop 8) Check nginx-rtmp access log (not just error log) 9) Try increasing chunk_size to 8192 or higher 10) Try adding buflen/max_streams directives
